gomodel:run-figure1/tph1/enabled_by.snippet: tph-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00006600; tryptophan hydroxylase in ADF neurons, synthesizes serotonin.

gomodel:run-figure1/mod1/enabled_by.snippet: mod-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00003386; serotonin-gated chloride channel in URX neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/ser6/enabled_by.snippet: ser-6 resolved to WB:WBGene00021897; octopamine receptor in AWB neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/tbh1/enabled_by.snippet: tbh-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00006541; tyramine beta-hydroxylase in RIC neurons, synthesizes octopamine.

gomodel:run-figure1/nhr76/enabled_by.snippet: nhr-76 resolved to WB:WBGene00015497; nuclear hormone receptor/transcription factor in intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1/atgl1/enabled_by.snippet: atgl-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00015484; adipose triglyceride lipase in intestinal lipid droplets.

gomodel:run-figure1/tph1/molecular_funct.snippet: tph-1 annotated to GO:0004510 tryptophan 5-monooxygenase activity (ISS), the rate-limiting enzyme in serotonin biosynthesis.

gomodel:run-figure1/mod1/molecular_funct.snippet: mod-1 annotated to GO:0160039 serotonin-gated chloride channel activity (IDA); it is the ligand-gated anion channel gated by serotonin in URX neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/ser6/molecular_funct.snippet: ser-6 annotated to GO:0004989 octopamine receptor activity (IDA); it is the GPCR responding to octopamine in AWB neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/tbh1/molecular_funct.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to GO:0004836 tyramine-beta hydroxylase activity (IMP); converts tyramine to octopamine in RIC neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/nhr76/molecular_func.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to GO:0003700 DNA-binding transcription factor activity (IEA); nuclear hormone receptor that positively regulates transcription by RNA Pol II (IMP).

gomodel:run-figure1/atgl1/molecular_func.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to GO:0004806 triacylglycerol lipase activity (IBA/IEA); the adipose triglyceride lipase that initiates lipolysis from lipid droplets in intestinal cells.

gomodel:run-figure1/tph1/part_of.snippet: tph-1 annotated to GO:0006587 serotonin biosynthetic process from L-tryptophan (IMP); tph-1 is required for serotonin production in ADF neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/mod1/part_of.snippet: mod-1 annotated to GO:1904123 positive regulation of fatty acid beta-oxidation by serotonin receptor signaling pathway (IMP); mod-1 in URX neurons couples serotonin sensing to downstream fat catabolism.

gomodel:run-figure1/ser6/part_of.snippet: ser-6 annotated to GO:1904122 positive regulation of fatty acid beta-oxidation by octopamine signaling pathway (IMP); octopamine receptor in AWB neurons contributes to fat catabolism via the neuroendocrine circuit.

gomodel:run-figure1/tbh1/part_of.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to GO:0006589 octopamine biosynthetic process (IMP); tbh-1 is required for octopamine production in RIC neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/nhr76/part_of.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to GO:1904123 positive regulation of fatty acid beta-oxidation by serotonin receptor signaling pathway (IMP); nuclear receptor in intestine that is the transcriptional effector of neuroendocrine signaling from neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/atgl1/part_of.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to GO:0016042 lipid catabolic process (IEA); ATGL-1 initiates triglyceride breakdown feeding fatty acids into beta-oxidation in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1/tph1/occurs_in.snippet: Figure panel E shows tph-1 inside the ADF neuron sub-compartment within NEURONS.
gomodel:run-figure1/tph1/occurs_in.justification: Panel E explicitly labels tph-1 as being associated with the ADF neuron compartment. No GO CC term for ADF neuron exists; using WormBase anatomy term WBbt:0006865.

gomodel:run-figure1/mod1/occurs_in.snippet: mod-1 annotated to GO:0005886 plasma membrane (IDA); ligand-gated ion channel localized at the plasma membrane of URX neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/ser6/occurs_in.snippet: ser-6 annotated to GO:0005886 plasma membrane (IBA/IEA); GPCR octopamine receptor localized at the plasma membrane of AWB neurons.

gomodel:run-figure1/tbh1/occurs_in.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to GO:0045202 synapse (IDA); tyramine-beta hydroxylase localizes to synapses in RIC neurons where octopamine is synthesized and released.

gomodel:run-figure1/nhr76/occurs_in.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to GO:0005634 nucleus (IEA/IBA); nuclear hormone receptor that functions in the intestinal cell nucleus to drive ATGL-1 transcription.

gomodel:run-figure1/atgl1/occurs_in.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to GO:0005811 lipid droplet (IDA/IBA); ATGL-1 localizes to intestinal lipid droplets where it catalyzes triglyceride lipolysis.

gomodel:run-figure1/tph1/causal/gomodel:.snippet: Noble et al. 2013 (Cell Metab) show that tph-1 in ADF neurons synthesizes serotonin (5-HT) that is released and acts on mod-1, the serotonin-gated chloride channel expressed in URX neurons, to initiate the fat-regulatory endocrine signal. Panel E arrow labeled '5-HT' from ADF (tph-1) to URX (mod-1) depicts this synaptic serotonin neurotransmission step.

gomodel:run-figure1/mod1/causal/gomodel:.snippet: Noble et al. 2013 show that serotonin receptor activation (mod-1) in URX neurons relays an endocrine signal (dashed arrow in panel E) from the neuronal compartment to the intestine, where nhr-76, a nuclear hormone receptor, is a transcriptional effector. This inter-tissue endocrine relay from URX to intestinal nhr-76 is a central finding of the paper.

gomodel:run-figure1/tbh1/causal/gomodel:.snippet: Noble et al. 2013 demonstrate that tbh-1 in RIC neurons synthesizes octopamine (Oct), which signals via the ser-6 octopamine receptor in AWB neurons to contribute to the integrated serotonin-octopamine circuit controlling fat. Panel E arrow labeled 'Oct' from RIC (tbh-1) to AWB (ser-6) depicts this octopaminergic neurotransmission.

gomodel:run-figure1/ser6/causal/gomodel:.snippet: Noble et al. 2013 show that octopamine signaling through ser-6 in AWB neurons is part of the integrated neuronal circuit that converges on the endocrine signal to the intestine. AWB (ser-6) is depicted as part of the NEURONS network feeding into the overall circuit that includes URX (mod-1) and the downstream endocrine relay; both octopamine and serotonin pathways cooperate to produce the endocrine signal controlling fat.

gomodel:run-figure1/nhr76/causal/gomodel.snippet: Palamiuc et al. 2017 (Nat Commun) and Noble et al. 2013 establish that nhr-76, a nuclear hormone receptor in intestinal cells, directly drives expression of atgl-1 (ATGL/adipose triglyceride lipase). Panel E explicitly shows an arrow from nhr-76 to ATGL inside the intestine compartment, indicating nhr-76 transcriptionally activates atgl-1 to initiate lipolysis and fat catabolism.

gomodel:run-figure1/atgl1/causal/gomodel.snippet: atgl-1 triacylglycerol lipase activity is causally upstream (positive effect) of intestinal fat loss / beta-oxidation, as shown by the arrow from ATGL to beta-oxidation/Fat loss in panel E. This enzymatic lipolysis step releases fatty acids for beta-oxidation and net fat reduction.
gomodel:run-figure1/atgl1/causal/gomodel.justification: Panel E explicitly shows an arrow from ATGL to 'beta-oxidation / Fat loss' inside the INTESTINE compartment. atgl-1 encodes the adipose triglyceride lipase that hydrolyzes triglycerides to release fatty acids which enter beta-oxidation. No separate activity node was created for beta-oxidation as a process; the nhr-76 node is the closest upstream intestinal effector, but this edge — atgl-1 promoting fat loss — is a biochemical consequence of its triacylglycerol lipase activity that is intrinsic to its annotated function (GO:0019433 triglyceride catabolic process; GO:0016042 lipid catabolic process). A second add_causal from atgl1 to itself is not meaningful; instead this step is best captured as part_of the lipid catabolic process set on the atgl1 activity node.
